title: Microscopy-based bead protein-protein interaction assay
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f6mi6","text":"This protocol describes how to perform microscopy-based bead protein-protein interaction assay with GST- or mCherry-tagged proteins as baits and fluorescently-tagged proteins as preys. The protocol requires to have purified proteins and allows to monitor protein-protein interaction in an equilibrium state. The fluorescent signal can be quantified.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"eg000","text":"Experiment should be repeated at least three times for statistical analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"brq8j","text":"Glutathione Sepharose 4B (Cytiva)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"99kas","text":" RFP-Trap Agarose beads (ChromoTek) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cn950","text":" 384-well glass-bottom microplate (Greiner Bio-One)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9f64f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6cr7f","text":"confocal microscope","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7a1th","text":"ImageJ software","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Equilibrate 20 µL Glutathione Sepharose 4B or RFP-Trap Agarose beads with 200 µL SEC buffer Prepare these bead equilibrations for each condition.
Incubate equilibrated beads with GST- or mCherry-tagged bait protein for a final concentration of 5 micromolar (µM) in SEC buffer for 01:00:00 at 4 °C with gentle rotation.
Centrifuge beads at 3000 rcf for 00:02:00 at 4 °C
Remove the supernatant and wash beads with 200 µL SEC buffer
Repeat for a total of 2 washes, then discard buffer
Add 20 µL SEC buffer to achieve a beads:buffer ratio of 1:1

Pipette prey proteins into the wells of a 384-well glass-bottom microplate (Greiner Bio-One) A volume of at least 20 µL should be used to cover the bottom of the well Prey concentration should be 0.1 micromolar (µM) to 1 micromolar (µM) , but should be adjusted depending on the strength of the interaction Different preys should be conjugated to different fluorophores
Pipette 1 µL of bait-coated beads into each well
Incubate the plate for 00:30:00 in the dark at Room temperature
Use a microscope configured to detect fluorescent signal (e.g. Zeiss LSM 700 confocal microscope equipped with Plan-Apochromat 20X/0.8 objective)
Acquire fluorescent images in the middle section of the beads and collect more than one image for each well
Also acquire bright field images for each field
Draw several lines across each bead in the fluorescent channel and measure the intensity along the lines
Record the maximum intensity for each bead
For background correction, measure the average intensity of a rectangular ROI that covers an area of each field of view with no beads
Subtract the average intensity of the background ROI from each bead maximum in that field
Calculate the average of the background-corrected maximum intensities of beads for each sample
creator: OLIVIA HARDING
